창세기 24:1.content: Abraham was old, and well advanced in age. Yahweh had blessed Abraham in all things.

창세기 24:2.content: Abraham said to his servant, the elder of his house, who ruled over all that he had, “Please put your hand under my thigh.

창세기 24:3.content: I will make you swear by Yahweh, the God of heaven and the God of the earth, that you shall not take a wife for my son of the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I live.

창세기 24:4.content: But you shall go to my country, and to my relatives, and take a wife for my son Isaac.”

창세기 24:5.content: The servant said to him, “What if the woman isn’t willing to follow me to this land? Must I bring your son again to the land you came from?”

창세기 24:6.content: Abraham said to him, “Beware that you don’t bring my son there again.

창세기 24:7.content: Yahweh, the God of heaven—who took me from my father’s house, and from the land of my birth, who spoke to me, and who swore to me, saying, ‘I will give this land to your offspring—he will send his angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.

창세기 24:8.content: If the woman isn’t willing to follow you, then you shall be clear from this oath to me. Only you shall not bring my son there again.”

창세기 24:9.content: The servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master, and swore to him concerning this matter.

창세기 24:10.content: The servant took ten of his master’s camels, and departed, having a variety of good things of his master’s with him. He arose, and went to Mesopotamia, to the city of Nahor.

창세기 24:11.content: He made the camels kneel down outside the city by the well of water at the time of evening, the time that women go out to draw water.

창세기 24:12.content: He said, “Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, please give me success today, and show kindness to my master Abraham.

창세기 24:13.content: Behold, I am standing by the spring of water. The daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.

창세기 24:14.content: Let it happen, that the young lady to whom I will say, ‘Please let down your pitcher, that I may drink,’ then she says, ‘Drink, and I will also give your camels a drink,’—let her be the one you have appointed for your servant Isaac. By this I will know that you have shown kindness to my master.”

창세기 24:15.content: Before he had finished speaking, behold, Rebekah came out, who was born to Bethuel the son of Milcah, the wife of Nahor, Abraham’s brother, with her pitcher on her shoulder.

창세기 24:16.content: The young lady was very beautiful to look at, a virgin. No man had known her. She went down to the spring, filled her pitcher, and came up.

창세기 24:17.content: The servant ran to meet her, and said, “Please give me a drink, a little water from your pitcher.”

창세기 24:18.content: She said, “Drink, my lord.” She hurried, and let down her pitcher on her hand, and gave him a drink.

창세기 24:19.content: When she had finished giving him a drink, she said, “I will also draw for your camels, until they have finished drinking.”

창세기 24:20.content: She hurried, and emptied her pitcher into the trough, and ran again to the well to draw, and drew for all his camels.

창세기 24:21.content: The man looked steadfastly at her, remaining silent, to know whether Yahweh had made his journey prosperous or not.

창세기 24:22.content: As the camels had done drinking, the man took a golden ring of half a shekel weight, and two bracelets for her hands of ten shekels weight of gold,

창세기 24:23.content: and said, “Whose daughter are you? Please tell me. Is there room in your father’s house for us to stay?”

창세기 24:24.content: She said to him, “I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.”

창세기 24:25.content: She said moreover to him, “We have both straw and feed enough, and room to lodge in.”

창세기 24:26.content: The man bowed his head, and worshiped Yahweh.

창세기 24:27.content: He said, “Blessed be Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, who has not forsaken his loving kindness and his truth toward my master. As for me, Yahweh has led me on the way to the house of my master’s relatives.”

창세기 24:28.content: The young lady ran, and told her mother’s house about these words.

창세기 24:29.content: Rebekah had a brother, and his name was Laban. Laban ran out to the man, to the spring.

창세기 24:30.content: When he saw the ring, and the bracelets on his sister’s hands, and when he heard the words of Rebekah his sister, saying, “This is what the man said to me,” he came to the man. Behold, he was standing by the camels at the spring.

창세기 24:31.content: He said, “Come in, you blessed of Yahweh. Why do you stand outside? For I have prepared the house, and room for the camels.”

창세기 24:32.content: The man came into the house, and he unloaded the camels. He gave straw and feed for the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.

창세기 24:33.content: Food was set before him to eat, but he said, “I will not eat until I have told my message.” Laban said, “Speak on.”

창세기 24:35.content: Yahweh has blessed my master greatly. He has become great. Yahweh has given him flocks and herds, silver and gold, male servants and female servants, and camels and donkeys.

창세기 24:36.content: Sarah, my master’s wife, bore a son to my master when she was old. He has given all that he has to him.

창세기 24:37.content: My master made me swear, saying, ‘You shall not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, in whose land I live,

창세기 24:38.content: but you shall go to my father’s house, and to my relatives, and take a wife for my son.’

창세기 24:39.content: I asked my master, ‘What if the woman will not follow me?’

창세기 24:40.content: He said to me, ‘Yahweh, before whom I walk, will send his angel with you, and prosper your way. You shall take a wife for my son from my relatives, and of my father’s house.

창세기 24:41.content: Then you will be clear from my oath, when you come to my relatives. If they don’t give her to you, you shall be clear from my oath.’

창세기 24:42.content: I came today to the spring, and said, ‘Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, if now you do prosper my way which I go—

창세기 24:43.content: behold, I am standing by this spring of water. Let it happen, that the maiden who comes out to draw, to whom I will say, “Please give me a little water from your pitcher to drink,”

창세기 24:44.content: then she tells me, “Drink, and I will also draw for your camels,”—let her be the woman whom Yahweh has appointed for my master’s son.’

창세기 24:45.content: Before I had finished speaking in my heart, behold, Rebekah came out with her pitcher on her shoulder. She went down to the spring, and drew. I said to her, ‘Please let me drink.’

창세기 24:46.content: She hurried and let down her pitcher from her shoulder, and said, ‘Drink, and I will also give your camels a drink.’ So I drank, and she also gave the camels a drink.

창세기 24:47.content: I asked her, and said, ‘Whose daughter are you?’ She said, ‘The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor’s son, whom Milcah bore to him.’ I put the ring on her nose, and the bracelets on her hands.

창세기 24:48.content: I bowed my head, and worshiped Yahweh, and blessed Yahweh, the God of my master Abraham, who had led me in the right way to take my master’s brother’s daughter for his son.

창세기 24:49.content: Now if you will deal kindly and truly with my master, tell me. If not, tell me, that I may turn to the right hand, or to the left.”

창세기 24:50.content: Then Laban and Bethuel answered, “The thing proceeds from Yahweh. We can’t speak to you bad or good.

창세기 24:51.content: Behold, Rebekah is before you. Take her, and go, and let her be your master’s son’s wife, as Yahweh has spoken.”

창세기 24:52.content: When Abraham’s servant heard their words, he bowed himself down to the earth to Yahweh.

창세기 24:53.content: The servant brought out jewels of silver, and jewels of gold, and clothing, and gave them to Rebekah. He also gave precious things to her brother and her mother.

창세기 24:54.content: They ate and drank, he and the men who were with him, and stayed all night. They rose up in the morning, and he said, “Send me away to my master.”

창세기 24:55.content: Her brother and her mother said, “Let the young lady stay with us a few days, at least ten. After that she will go.”

창세기 24:56.content: He said to them, “Don’t hinder me, since Yahweh has prospered my way. Send me away that I may go to my master.”

창세기 24:57.content: They said, “We will call the young lady, and ask her.”

창세기 24:58.content: They called Rebekah, and said to her, “Will you go with this man?” She said, “I will go.”

창세기 24:59.content: They sent away Rebekah, their sister, with her nurse, Abraham’s servant, and his men.

창세기 24:60.content: They blessed Rebekah, and said to her, “Our sister, may you be the mother of thousands of ten thousands, and let your offspring possess the gate of those who hate them.”

창세기 24:61.content: Rebekah arose with her ladies. They rode on the camels, and followed the man. The servant took Rebekah, and went his way.

창세기 24:62.content: Isaac came from the way of Beer Lahai Roi, for he lived in the land of the South.

창세기 24:63.content: Isaac went out to meditate in the field at the evening. He lifted up his eyes and looked. Behold, there were camels coming.

창세기 24:64.content: Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she got off the camel.

창세기 24:65.content: She said to the servant, “Who is the man who is walking in the field to meet us?” The servant said, “It is my master.” She took her veil, and covered herself.

창세기 24:66.content: The servant told Isaac all the things that he had done.

창세기 24:67.content: Isaac brought her into his mother Sarah’s tent, and took Rebekah, and she became his wife. He loved her. So Isaac was comforted after his mother’s death.
